title: Hunt and fix the nested-clone verify suite that rewrote the parent repo git origin
action: P1 filed 2026-07-20: running run_verify.mjs inside a clone nested at .bee/spikes/<clone> rewrote the PARENT repo remote.origin.url to the spike path (and spike dirs vanished after runs). Hunt: grep all suites for git remote set-url / remote add / config remote and for upward root resolution (git rev-parse --show-toplevel or resolveRoots walking up) executed with a cwd that can escape the suite own fixture — candidates: installer E2E suites, worktree CLI/store tests, onboarding tests. Reproduce: create a scratch git repo with a known origin, clone the beegog repo nested inside it, run the suspect suite(s) from the nested clone, diff the parent git config. Fix the culprit: pin its cwd/root to the fixture (pass explicit root, never resolve upward past the fixture boundary). Add a regression test (new scripts/test_nested_clone_isolation.mjs, auto-discovered by run_verify): build parent-with-origin fixture, run the previously-guilty operation from the nested clone, assert parent .git/config is byte-identical before/after.

truths: The culprit suite and exact operation are named with file:line in the done-report
Regression test proves parent remotes stay byte-identical
prohibitions: Never run the reproduction against the real checkout .git — fixtures only

trace.outcome: Hunted the P1: grepped every .mjs in scripts/, skills/, .bee/bin/, hooks/ for git-remote-mutating operations (remote/set-url/config remote, any case) -- ZERO matches anywhere in the repo. Empirically reproduced the incident's exact layout in fixtures (parent git repo with a known origin, an independent `git clone` nested at <parent>/.bee/spikes/<name>, matching the leftover .bee/spikes/clean-clone/.git/config artifact still sitting in this repo whose own origin points at the real checkout) and ran every named-candidate suite (test_resolveroots_p40, test_worktree_grant_resolve, test_worktree_store, test_worktree_cli 135/135, test_worktree_holds, test_worktree_holds_race, test_installers_e2e 23/23, skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs) plus a FULL 53-suite node scripts/run_verify.mjs run from inside the nested clone -- none corrupted the parent fixture's .git/config or removed the nested clone. No script-level culprit reproduces against current code. Added scripts/test_nested_clone_isolation.mjs (auto-discovered, 16/16 passing) that pins the real invariant instead: resolveRoots, createFeatureWorktree, mergeFeatureWorktree, and a cwd-pinned `bee status` all leave a parent repo's remotes byte-identical when run from a clone nested inside its own tree. Root cause is most likely procedural (a multi-step manual git setup for a 'clean clone' hermetic verify, where a cd in one shell invocation did not carry into a later, separate git remote command), not a code defect -- documented in the test file's header and the done-report.
trace.friction: Cell hypothesized a specific culprit test suite performing git remote set-url/upward root resolution with an escaping cwd. Exhaustive grep (zero hits for 'remote' in any .mjs repo-wide) and full empirical reproduction (all named candidates + full 53-suite verify run from a nested clone fixture) found no such suite; current code is clean. Leftover .bee/spikes/clean-clone/.git/config (origin = parent checkout path) is the real incident artifact and points to a procedural cwd-persistence mistake during manual release-flow git setup, not a script bug. No culprit file:line exists to name because none was found despite the hunt described in the cap trace.
